questions.question: What is the primary purpose of removing navigation menus from a landing page?

options.text: To make the page load faster.
To save space for more text.
To keep the visitor focused on a single goal and prevent them from clicking away.
Because navigation menus don't work on mobile devices.

questions.explanation: Landing pages are 'one-way streets.' Removing navigation reduces distractions and forces the visitor to either convert or leave.

questions.question: Which of these headlines follows the 'Specific Result' formula?

options.text: Welcome to our innovative marketing platform.
Double your sales calls in 30 days without spending more on ads.
The best CRM for small businesses.
Stop struggling with your business operations today.

questions.explanation: Formula: [Achieve X] in [Timeframe] without [Frustration]. Option B clearly defines the result, time, and removed obstacle.

questions.question: According to the lesson, what is the best way to design a CTA button to maximize clicks?

options.text: Make it the same color as the background for a sleek look.
Use generic copy like 'Submit'.
Use a contrasting color and value-oriented copy like 'Get My Free Guide'.
Hide it at the very bottom of the page only.

questions.explanation: High-performing CTAs use contrasting colors to stand out and copy that emphasizes the value the user receives from clicking.

questions.question: What does 'Above the Fold' mean in landing page design?

options.text: The portion of the page visible before the user scrolls.
The section where the testimonials are located.
The footer of the website.
The code used to build the page.

questions.explanation: This is the most critical area of your page, as it's the first thing every visitor sees.

questions.question: Which of these is considered a 'Conversion Killer' by the lesson?

options.text: Including a product screenshot.
Having a page that takes 10 seconds to load.
Using bullet points for scannability.
Adding an FAQ section at the bottom.

questions.explanation: Slow load times significantly decrease conversion rates as visitors lose patience and leave.

questions.question: Choose an existing page on your site or a page you intended to build. Apply the 'What if I...' test to your current (or planned) CTA button. What is the value-oriented copy you would write for that button? Then, identify one element you will remove from your page to reduce distraction.
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate the student's CTA copy and distraction reduction strategy. Check if the new CTA copy is truly value-driven. Suggest one specific 'Risk Reduction' phrase they could add near their new button.
